//: Fas 4b 8b.4a — Asset A. Maps the JobTech taxonomy's occupation-FIELDS (yrkesområden)
//1: onto the four branschgrupper Klas ruled on 2026-07-13, and gives each branschgrupp its
//2: section rule-table (design handoff §7). Read by BranschgruppProvider; consumed by the
//3: GetCvSectionSuggestions read-slice. NO AI/LLM (ADR 0071) — a static, versioned table.
//5: ALL 21 occupation-fields are listed EXPLICITLY, including the 17 that map to 'ovriga'.
//6: This is deliberate and is the antidote to the vacuous-filter failure mode (#805-3): a
//7: 3-entries-plus-a-default table cannot tell 'this field means Övriga' apart from 'this
//8: field is unknown to us'. Listing all 21 makes the completeness test possible — it pins
//9: the conceptId SET against taxonomy-snapshot.json in BOTH directions, so a JobTech
//10: snapshot bump that adds/replaces a field goes RED here instead of silently sending
//11: every CV to Övriga. The 'label' is maintainer documentation only and is NOT asserted:
//12: the mapping is keyed on conceptId, so an upstream label rewording is harmless and must

occupationFields.//: Klas ruling C3(ii) 2026-07-13, DECLINED: 'Yrken med teknisk inriktning' stays in
occupationFields.//1: Övriga. IT/tech's rules are IT-specific — proposing a GitHub link and an AWS
occupationFields.//2: certificate to a byggnadsingenjör is the machine-slop impression the civic tone
occupationFields.//3: exists to prevent. Coverage bought by mis-suggesting is negative value.
occupationFields.label: Yrken med teknisk inriktning

//14: The rule table (design handoff §7). 'heading' is the Swedish heading that gets written
//15: INTO the user's CV when a suggestion is accepted — it is document CONTENT, not UI chrome,
//16: which is why it lives here and not in messages/sv.json. Every heading MUST be recognisable
//17: by the parsing lexicon (BranschgruppProvider fails loud at startup if it is not, and the
//18: contract test pins it): a heading the segmenter cannot see is a section whose body gets
//19: swallowed by the preceding one on the next import — the live #815 bug PR-1 fixed.
//20: 'rationale' is the badge copy (parity ProposedChange.Rationale: KB-sourced, never
//21: synthesised prose). Klas ruling: 'Referenser' is never suggested (a section whose only
//22: sanctioned content is 'Lämnas på begäran' is close to worthless).

branschgrupper.//: Handoff §7 lists Projekt under 'Visas ej som förslag' for vård. That is expressed by
branschgrupper.//1: ABSENCE — Projekt is simply not in the suggestion list above. There is deliberately no
branschgrupper.//2: suppression FIELD: nothing is inherited from a base set, so a subtraction could never
branschgrupper.//3: subtract anything. Its only possible outcomes would be 'no effect' or 'crash at
branschgrupper.//4: startup' — a trap for the next editor, not a guard. Mutation testing proved it:
branschgrupper.//5: deleting the filter that read it broke no test, because it could never fire.
branschgrupper.//6: Rule (a) holds regardless: a Projekt section the user already WROTE is kept and shown.
branschgrupper.//7: The engine never HIDES the user's own content — it only declines to suggest.

branschgrupper.//: Handoff §7 names 'Meriteringsnivå' here. DROPPED (8b.4a engineering call, not a
branschgrupper.//1: product change): it has no sectionId, and adding one would mean teaching the parsing
branschgrupper.//2: lexicon to treat 'Meriteringsnivå' as a section boundary on every CV import — a live
branschgrupper.//3: recognition change. PR-1's bar for a lexicon entry is evidence that real CVs carry the
branschgrupper.//4: heading and lose text without it (legitimation/korkort had it). Meriteringsnivå has
branschgrupper.//5: none: it is a Skolverket career step (förstelärare/lektor) — an attribute of a post,
branschgrupper.//6: belonging inside an Erfarenhet entry or under Lärarlegitimation, not a top-level CV
branschgrupper.//7: heading. Skola loses nothing: the handoff's other two concepts are already expressible
branschgrupper.//8: ('lärarlegitimation' → legitimation, 'fortbildning' → kurser). Reversible in two lines
branschgrupper.//9: (one lexicon synonym block + one entry here) the day we have the evidence.

branschgrupper.//: The 62.1 % majority experience (measured against 93 469 ads), NOT a hole. It is a
branschgrupper.//1: first-class row with its own suggestions. Note the two distinct empty states the
branschgrupper.//2: read-slice keeps apart: a user with NO stated occupation lands here AND is asked for
branschgrupper.//3: one; a user WHOSE stated occupation resolves here is NOT asked again — she already
